The psalmist sincerely asks God to protect him from troubling fear. The tongue is a small part of the body, yet it makes great claims. Righteous people are the targets that wicked people aim at; they cannot speak peaceably about or to them. There is no defense against a lying tongue. It is bad to do wrong, but worse to encourage ourselves and each other in doing it. It shows that someone’s heart is completely hardened when it is fully determined to do evil. A practical disbelief in God's knowledge of everything underlies every wickedness. The benefit of a good cause and a clear conscience appears most clearly when nothing can help a person against their enemies except God alone, who is always ready to help.

When God brings the troubles upon people that they have wished on others, it is enough to condemn them to the deepest hell. Those who love cursing will experience it themselves. Those who see this should understand and observe God’s hand in everything; unless we do so, we are unlikely to benefit from God's providence. Righteous people will be glad in the Lord; not glad of the misery and ruin of their fellow humans, but glad that God is glorified, his word is fulfilled, and the cause of wronged innocence is pleaded effectively. They rejoice not in people, nor in themselves, nor in any creation or enjoyment, nor in their wisdom, strength, wealth, or righteousness; but in Christ, in whom all the descendants of Israel are justified and find glory, and in what he is to them, and has done for them.
